text:
> **Don't ask the model to classify authorship — derive it mechanically from which transcript turn the evidence range falls in.** User turn → user claim. Agent claim → only enters the spec if a *later user turn ratifies it* (and that ratifying turn is the cited evidence).

This is Rust-checkable (the evidence range already identifies the speaker), and it even handles the red-team's tricky cases correctly: a user quoting the agent's proposal back *is* ratification → user intent; an unratified agent assertion → never becomes spec. That's the failure mode (confident agent hallucination accreting as spec) that *none* of the embedding/diff approaches touch. [...] not really; what an agent said/infered IS part of what is captured -- it's just subject to be removed when a correction lands later, but it's ok, even better if the wiki distinguies what are explicit directions vs implicit directions for the product ("add oauth integration" -> explicit direction "I will add google oauth support too" -> implicit until the user ackwnoledges -- if later the user says "only support github oauth" then the google oauth support product direction (agent-generated) gets deleted and the explicit "only github oauth" gets codified as made permanent)
